announcements.title: Product improvements and bug fixes
announcements.desc: This release improves the Windows 365 sign-in experience, connection reliability, and accessibility. You see 'update in progress' message instead of a black screen during reboot. It also adds more regional keyboard support and fixes issues with sign-in, Quick Settings, and overall reliability.

announcements.title: Recover Windows 365 Enterprise Cloud PCs that were Deprovisioned due to License Expiration (preview)
announcements.desc: You can now recover Window 365 Enterprise Cloud PCs that were deprovisioned due to license expiration. Once you repurchase the expired licenses and ensure you have followed the prerequisites, you can use the retention snapshot to restore the Cloud PC for your end user. For more information, seePoint-in-time restore for Windows 365 Enterprise.

announcements.desc: Remote Desktop Protocol (RDP) Multipath is now generally available for Azure Virtual Desktop and Windows 365. announcements.desc: Windows 365 is enhancing Cloud PC security by having clipboard, drive, opaque low-level USB, and printer redirections disabled by default for all newly provisioned and reprovisioned Cloud PCs. announcements.title: Version 11.0.0.33

announcements.title: New Connection Center

announcements.title: Two new display language packs for Cloud PCs

announcements.title: Intelligent pre-start for Windows 365 Frontline in dedicated mode (preview)
announcements.desc: Windows 365 Frontline Cloud PCs have the ability to predict when a user connects and pre-start their Cloud PC before they connect. This prediction improves connect times for such users' Cloud PCs. For more information, seeIntelligent pre-start for Windows 365 Frontline in dedicated mode.

announcements.title: Configure non-UTC time zones for bulk reprovisioning for Windows 365 Frontline Cloud PCs in shared mode
announcements.desc: You can now configure non-UTC time zones when scheduling bulk reprovisioning for Windows 365 Frontline Cloud PCs in shared mode. For more information, seeSchedule bulk reprovision Frontline Cloud PCs in shared mode.

announcements.title: Cross region disaster recovery for Windows 365 Frontline in dedicated mode (preview)
announcements.desc: Windows 365 Frontline in dedicated mode now supports cross region disaster recovery. For more information, seeCross region disaster recovery in Windows 365.

On the Connection Center screen, users can: Choose which of their Cloud PCs to connect to. Troubleshoot connection errors. Reboot and restore their Cloud PCs.

Fixed an issue that could sometimes prevent Task Manager from launching correctly (57115154). Fixed issue with inadvertent timeouts on certain actions in the security key sign-in process (57007130).

For more information, seeEnable GPU acceleration for Azure Virtual Desktop.

announcements.title: High Efficiency Video Coding (H.265) hardware acceleration support
announcements.desc: Windows 365 GPU-enabled Super and Max SKUs now support graphics processing unit (GPU) acceleration for frame encoding using HEVC/H.265. GPU acceleration improves graphical experiences when using the Remote Desktop Protocol (RDP) with a compatible GPU-enabled Cloud PCs. announcements.title: Windows 365 Frontline dedicated mode concurrency management now generally available
announcements.desc: Windows 365 Frontline dedicated mode concurrency management has moved out of preview and into generally availability. For more information, seeConcurrency management.

announcements.title: Teams VDI 1.0 support on iOS(preview)
announcements.desc: Windows 365 now supports Teams VDI 1.0 on iOS. For more information, seeMicrosoft Teams on a Cloud PC.

announcements.title: Cloud PC utilization report: new options for aggregated time connected
announcements.desc: You can now: Pick time spans of 28, 60, and 90 days for the aggregated time connected data. See Cloud PCs that no one has connected to yet. For more information, seeCloud PC utilization report.

announcements.title: Cloud PC action status report batch progress now generally available
announcements.desc: The following feature has moved out of preview and into general availability: The Cloud PC action status report shows batches of devices on which actions were triggered. You can see the batch current progress. For more information, seeCloud PC actions report.

announcements.title: windows365.microsoft.com being replaced by Windows App
announcements.desc: The windows365.microsoft.com end user portal is currently being deprecated. Navigation to windows365.microsoft.com is automatically redirected to Windows App on the web. This deprecation will be complete on June 1, 2025. (Windows 365 Government users can still access the end user portal.)

announcements.title: Resize Windows 365 Frontline Cloud PCs in dedicated mode (preview)

announcements.desc: You can now use a Conditional Access policy to require token protection for sign-in tokens (refresh tokens) on Windows devices. Such policies can reduce attacks using token theft by ensuring a token is usable only from the intended device. announcements.desc: You can now see the health status of Cloud PC restore points before deciding to start a restore. For more information, seeRestore a single Cloud PC to a previous stateandRestore multiple Cloud PCs in bulk.

announcements.desc: The Connected Frontline Cloud PCs report now shows a user's session length. You can also restart Windows 365 Frontline Cloud PCs from the report if you've reached max concurrency on any individual assignments. For more information, seeConnected Frontline Cloud PCs report.

The latter allocates a second Cloud PC at the time of configuration which improves RTO. As the recovery Cloud PC already exists, there isn't a capacity risk at the time of failure. For more information, seeWindows 365 disaster recovery plus.

announcements.title: Data disk not allowed for custom images
announcements.desc: Uploading a custom image with attached managed data disks are no longer allowed for Windows 365 custom images.

announcements.title: Windows 365 Frontline Cloud PCs in shared mode now generally available
